scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every GET list/detail resource documented by Castor EDC's own published Python client (reiniervlinschoten/castoredc_api, the closest available authoritative source of truth since the interactive Swagger UI at /api requires an authenticated session) is now a stream, and every practical, non-destructive, non-file-upload mutation is a writes.json action. study-scoped resources (record/field/form/site/role/survey/etc.) require the new study_id config value, matching legacy castoredc_api's CastorClient.link_study(study_id) session-scoping model.

excluded.reason: single-study detail is a strict subset of the study list stream's fields; the list stream already covers every study the account can access
single-user detail is a strict subset of the account-level user list stream
single-country detail is a strict subset of the country list stream
single-record detail is a strict subset of the records list stream
single-field detail is a strict subset of the fields list stream
single-field-dependency detail is a strict subset of the field_dependencies list stream
single-field-optiongroup detail is a strict subset of the field_optiongroups list stream
single-field-validation detail is a strict subset of the field_validations list stream
single-site detail is a strict subset of the sites list stream
single-metadata detail is a strict subset of the study_metadata list stream
single-metadatatype detail is a strict subset of the metadata_types list stream
single-phase detail is a strict subset of the phases list stream
single-query detail is a strict subset of the queries list stream
single-report detail is a strict subset of the reports list stream
sub-resource of a single report's step definitions; no legacy parity and low value versus the already-covered reports stream
single-report-instance detail is a strict subset of the report_instances list stream
per-record report-instance listing is a filtered view of the study-wide report_instances stream; the study-wide stream already surfaces every instance with its owning record_id field

bulk variant of create_report_instance (creates N instances in one call from a nested array body); the single-instance create_report_instance write covers the same mutation one record at a time and the engine's declarative write dialect has no per-record-array-of-objects body shape to express the bulk variant
single-role detail is a strict subset of the roles list stream
single-step detail is a strict subset of the steps list stream
single-survey detail is a strict subset of the surveys list stream
sub-resource of a single survey's step definitions; no legacy parity and low value versus the already-covered surveys stream
single-survey-package detail is a strict subset of the survey_packages list stream
single-survey-package-instance detail is a strict subset of the survey_package_instances list stream
lock/unlock toggle on an existing survey package instance; a narrow administrative state-flip with no read-side representation to round-trip against, deferred pending real demand
updates only the started_on timestamp of an existing survey package instance; narrow administrative correction, deferred pending real demand
single-study-user detail is a strict subset of the study_users list stream

invites an external person by email into the study with configurable manage/institute permissions; an account-provisioning action distinct from ordinary clinical data mutation, requiring elevated study-admin scope beyond this connector's data read/write risk profile
revokes a user's access to the study; destructive account-provisioning action, out of scope
changes a user's manage/site permissions on the study; account-provisioning action requiring elevated study-admin scope
returns aggregate study-level counters (record/site/user totals), not enumerable per-record data; no stable primary key for a stream row
returns a CSV export (Content-Type: text/csv) of the full study dataset, not a JSON list envelope this bundle's declarative HTTP dialect can paginate/project
returns a CSV export of the study's form/field structure, same non-JSON-envelope limitation as export/data
returns a CSV export of the study's option groups, same non-JSON-envelope limitation as export/data

every study-level data point across every record; the same field values are reachable per-record via the records stream's own data, and this collection-wide variant returns an unbounded fan-out with no per-record primary key of its own distinct from record_id+field_id already implicit in records
study-wide report data-point collection; would require per-record-instance fan-out with no stable single-request pagination shape documented, deferred pending real demand
study-wide survey data-point collection; same fan-out limitation as data-point-collection/report-instance
single report-instance's data points; needs a per-instance fan_out over report_instances with no documented list-pagination envelope of its own, deferred pending real demand
single survey-instance's data points; same per-instance fan-out limitation
single survey-package-instance's data points; same per-instance fan-out limitation

per-record study data points; a fan_out over records is possible in principle but the resulting per-field-value rows carry clinical trial subject-level data whose schema is entirely study-form-defined (unknowable ahead of time), the exact SCHEMA_AMBIGUOUS shape this dialect cannot safely project without a per-study dynamic_schema capability this bundle does not declare
per-record report data points; same study-form-defined dynamic schema limitation as data-point-collection/study
same dynamic schema limitation as data-point-collection/study, narrowed to one report
per-record survey data points; same study-form-defined dynamic schema limitation
same dynamic schema limitation, narrowed to one survey instance
same dynamic schema limitation, narrowed to one survey package instance

writes an arbitrary study-form-defined field_id/field_value pair; the mutation body shape depends entirely on that study's own dynamically-defined form fields, which this bundle cannot express as a fixed record_schema
same study-form-defined dynamic body-shape limitation as data-point-collection/study writes
same study-form-defined dynamic body-shape limitation
same study-form-defined dynamic body-shape limitation
same study-form-defined dynamic schema limitation as data-point-collection/study
single study field value for a record; same dynamic schema limitation, narrowed to one field
writes a single study field value; body shape is the field's own study-defined type, same dynamic-body limitation as the data-point-collection writes
same study-form-defined dynamic schema limitation, scoped to one report instance's fields
single report field value; same dynamic schema limitation
writes a single report field value or an uploaded file; dynamic body shape plus optional multipart file upload, out of this dialect's scope
same study-form-defined dynamic schema limitation, scoped to one survey instance's fields
single survey field value; same dynamic schema limitation
writes a single survey field value or an uploaded file; same dynamic body shape plus optional multipart file upload limitation
single-record randomization detail; a narrow read with no list envelope, low standalone value once create_randomization covers the mutation itself
per-record mobile-app pairing token, not clinical trial data; narrow device-integration feature deferred pending real demand
creates a per-record device-pairing token, same narrow device-integration scope as the read
updates a per-record device-pairing token, same narrow device-integration scope
deletes a per-record device-pairing token, same narrow device-integration scope
electronic-consent status for a record; narrow compliance sub-feature with its own separate lifecycle, deferred pending real demand
creates econsent status for a record, same narrow scope as the read
updates econsent status for a record, same narrow scope
the OAuth2 client-credentials token exchange itself, fully modeled by base.auth's oauth2_client_credentials mode, not a data endpoint
